zh.title: 比特币/美元技术分析：震荡偏强高位整理延续
zh.content:
当前BTC/美元维持高位窄幅震荡结构，价格运行于61400附近，贴近24小时高点，整体仍站稳61000上方均线，短线多头动能未破坏，呈现高位整理偏强格局

MACD柱值463维持正区间，动能偏多但未明显扩张，RSI暂缺但结合价格贴近高点判断，市场进入上涨后的整理阶段而非趋势加速阶段

下方支撑关注61055均线及61300密集成交区，上方阻力在61479前高与61500整数关口，若有效突破则打开进一步上行空间，否则维持区间反复

操作上建议高位震荡思路，回踩61050至61300区间分批布局多单，突破站稳61500可看向62000区域，跌破60900则需及时控制风险离场

en.title: Bitcoin vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
Bitcoin vs US Dollar is currently exhibiting a tight consolidation phase around the 61,400 level after a marginal 24-hour decline of 0.03%. Price action remains compressed between 61,337 and 61,479, indicating equilibrium between buyers and sellers. The market shows reduced volatility following recent directional moves, with participants likely awaiting a catalyst for breakout. Overall structure remains mildly constructive as price holds above short-term average support.

Bitcoin is trading above its 20-period simple moving average at 61,055, reinforcing a short-term bullish bias despite the current pause in momentum. The MACD reading of 463.11 suggests positive underlying momentum, although the absence of a signal line limits confirmation. With RSI unavailable, momentum assessment relies primarily on price structure and moving average alignment, which still favors buyers maintaining control above dynamic support levels.

Immediate support is located near the SMA20 at 61,055, with stronger demand expected around the psychological 61,000 level if selling pressure increases. On the upside, resistance is defined by the recent high at 61,479, and a sustained break above this zone could open the path toward 62,000. The narrow trading range suggests compression, increasing the probability of an expansion move in either direction.

Strategy should focus on range trading while monitoring for breakout confirmation. Traders may consider buying near support around 61,000–61,055 with tight risk controls, targeting resistance at 61,479 and potentially 62,000 on continuation. Alternatively, a confirmed breakout above resistance could signal momentum entry positions, while a breakdown below 61,000 would shift bias toward defensive positioning and short opportunities. Volatility expansion is expected after consolidation.
